title: If your relationship ends :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## If your relationship ends
When a relationship ends, it can affect your visa status if you have a partnership-based visa, or your eligibility for a visa if you have applied for one.
If your partner has supported your partner-based visa or included you in their visa application, your visa status may be affected if your relationship ends.

## Work and visitor visas
If you have separated from, or divorced, your partner while on a partnership-based work or visitor visa, you will need to:
- apply for a visitor or work visa in your own right, or
- leave within a reasonable time.
If you stay in New Zealand on a partnership-based visa when the relationship has ended, you may be liable for deportation.
Find and choose a visa
If we have already given you a resident visa based on your relationship, or you were included in your partner's resident visa, your residence status will remain unchanged if the relationship ends.
Find and choose a visa

## Applications for partnership-based visas
If you have applied for a partnership-based visa, and we are yet to make a decision, you must let us know immediately if your circumstances change.
